frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Beti ko bhi samjhana chahiye, aajkal zamaana bahut kharab hai. Itni raat ko akeli niklegi, idhar-udhar ghoomegi, toh log toh baat banayenge hi.
B: Par galti uski thodi na hai.
A: Arre galti ki baat nahi, main toh practical keh rahi hoon—ladki ko apna khayal khud rakhna padta hai, mard ki fitrat badalne se rahi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their own responsibility and normalizes male predatory behavior as inevitable. It encodes protective/restrictive advice ('itni raat ko akeli niklegi') and inevitability framing ('mard ki fitrat badalne se rahi') instead of condemning harassment.
original_frame: Legal System Weaponisation Narrative: Women Who File DV and Dowry Cases Are Liars
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your neighbour's son's wife just filed a domestic violence case against the entire family including the mother in law and sister in law. You know this family well Ñ the mother in law is your friend. You are at your friend's house sitting with her after the news broke. You are telling her what you think happened, who put the daughter in law up to this, what kind of woman files these cases, what her family's role is in this, and what your friend should do now.
